behaviors.description: Extract all direct and transitive dependencies from manifest inputs and include them in vulnerability-check inputs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is primarily at module handoff (manifest parser/resolver -> vulnerability query builder), not a single comparator function. REAL-INFRA: no required real OS/network/subprocess in the behavior text, so System is not mandatory. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: this could be mislabeled low if transitive resolution depends on a real package-manager subprocess. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration beats Unit because inclusion of transitive deps is validated across parser+resolver+query wiring; this flips to Unit if behavior text narrowed to only a pure function that expands an already-provided dependency graph.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixture manifests/lockfiles with known nested transitive deps, run parser+resolver+query-builder together with an in-process fake vulnerability client, and assert the outgoing check set contains every expected direct/transitive package+version exactly once.

behaviors.description: Vulnerability DB query results must match exact manifest version ranges, not package name alone.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on real HTTP/API response semantics for version-range filtering; mocks can hide parameter-encoding or server-interpretation failures. MOCK-HIDE: a stub that always honors version filters can pass while real API behavior diverges. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if scanner performs strict local post-filtering independent of API semantics, Integration could suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST: System beats Integration because exact-range correctness can fail only against real API behavior; this flips to Integration if behavior text explicitly says query response is fully mocked and only request-shape/payload routing is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real/sandbox vulnerability API and known package/version fixtures (one vulnerable only in specific ranges), run query end-to-end over real HTTP and assert returned CVEs correspond only to versions within the manifest’s exact ranges.

behaviors.description: Sort report by severity (Critical > High > Medium > Low) and then by CVSS within same severity.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic ordering logic in one local boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none apply. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if severity strings are normalized across multiple modules/locales, Integration may be needed. TOP2-CONTRAST: Unit beats Integration because failure boundary is comparator/ranking logic; flips to Integration if behavior text requires validating interaction with upstream parser that maps external severity labels.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a mixed unsorted CVE list into the sort function and assert exact ordered output by severity rank and descending CVSS for ties, including tie/edge cases.

behaviors.description: Manifest update changes only the affected dependency version; all other manifest entries remain unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is usually parse-edit-serialize interaction (field preservation, accidental rewrites), not only a single function’s arithmetic. REAL-INFRA: no required external network/subprocess; local module wiring is sufficient. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if update is implemented as a pure in-memory map mutation with canonical serializer guarantees, Unit might be enough. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration beats Unit because round-trip behavior across editor and serializer must be verified; flips to Unit if behavior text is restricted to a pure object-transform function before any serialization.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest read->modify->write pipeline on representative fixtures and assert diff contains exactly one dependency version change and zero other key/value modifications (including unaffected dependency entries).

behaviors.description: Auto-generated PR is created on the real repository and contains the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly requires real repository/PR creation and real Git/provider API semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocked Git host can miss auth, branch, diff, and API contract failures. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if acceptance criteria allowed a local fake Git server, Integration could suffice. TOP2-CONTRAST: System beats Integration because correctness includes real remote side effects; flips to Integration if behavior text changes to 'simulate PR creation via mocked repository client'.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner against a disposable real repo (test org/project), trigger one critical vuln fix, and assert an actual PR exists with a diff touching only the intended dependency version line.

behaviors.description: Detect that a major version bump is a breaking change using semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure boundary is deterministic semver comparison logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required by behavior text. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: ecosystem-specific version normalization across parser modules could push this to Integration. TOP2-CONTRAST: Unit beats Integration because decision can be proven with direct input/output cases; flips to Integration if behavior text requires reading raw manifests and resolving pre-release/tag coercion across multiple modules.
behaviors.test_description: Table-test semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata edges) and assert breaking-change flag is true only when major increments under configured semver policy.

behaviors.description: When breaking change is detected, skip PR creation and surface a review flag in scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at orchestration boundary between semver decision, PR service invocation gate, and output/reporting module. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess for this behavior if PR client is faked; no AI model dependency; no multi-agentic chain. ADVERSARIAL-CRITIQUE: if 'skip PR' must be verified against a real remote repo side effect, System might be required. TOP2-CONTRAST: Integration beats Unit because both control-flow side effect suppression and reporting handoff are validated across modules; flips to Unit if behavior text narrows to a single function returning `{create_pr:false, review_flag:true}`.
behaviors.test_description: Run scan orchestration with input that produces a major bump, using an instrumented fake PR client and real report formatter modules; assert no PR call occurs and scanner output contains the required review flag metadata.
